t_ch: 啊，我已经迫不及待要与你展开一段如此美妙的旅程了！
t_en: Oh, I can't wait to begin a journey as magnificent as that with you!

t_ch: 别，我还不想死。
t_en: It's too early for me to die.

t_ch: 听起来有点吓人…
t_en: Sounds terrifying to me...

t_ch: 什么嘛。果然人类都不可信，嘴上说着越快越好，心里却怕得要命。
t_en: What? Humans are indeed deceitful. You claim to enjoy the speed, but you're actually scared to death deep down.

t_ch: 还要等多久，才能遇到真正懂我的人呢？
t_en: When will I meet someone who truly understands me?

t_ch: 还要等多久，才能遇到真正懂我的人呢？
t_en: When will I meet someone who truly understands me?

t_ch: 哔哔哔哔——噗！我是疾风侠！疾风——力量！
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Poof! I'm Quicksilver! Speed is power!

t_ch: 嗨，这位朋友，要是遇到了困难，请随时来找我。无论什么时候，疾风侠都是弱者最好的伙伴。梦境的和平与公义，就由我来守护。
t_en: Please come to me if you're in trouble, my friend. Quicksilver will always be the companion of the weak, no matter what happens. I'll maintain the peace and justice in the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 不太妙啊。你看起来愁眉苦脸的，发生了什么事吗？
t_en: I sense a negative vibe here, and you seem troubled. What's the matter?

t_ch: …我的钱不够用了。
t_en: ...I'm running out of cash.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我还以为是什么事呢。不用担心，疾风侠会替你解决的。
t_en: Hahaha, I thought it was the end of the world! Relax, I'll help you.

t_ch: 来。大声呼喊出我的名字！疾风侠，疾风——力量！
t_en: Come on! Just shout my name! Quicksilver. Speed is power!

t_ch: 疾风——力量！
t_en: Speed is power!

t_ch: 疾风——力量！！！
t_en: Speed is power!!!

t_ch: 很有气势！好，这个鼓胀的钱包就归你了，快打开看看吧。
t_en: I love your energy! This stuffed wallet is now yours. Check it out now!

t_ch: 不用感谢我，做好事是疾风侠的职责！
t_en: There's no need to thank me. Offering help is my job!

t_ch: 这是你赠送给我的吗？
t_en: Is this a gift for me?

t_ch: 这是我能免费拿的吗？
t_en: Is this something I can take for free?

t_ch: 嗯！不用担心，朋友，现在它就属于你。相信那位把钱包落下的乘客，也会很愿意帮助像你一样的弱者！
t_en: By all means! It now belongs to you, my friend. I'm sure that the passenger who left his wallet behind would be happy to help a needy like you!

t_ch: 所以我先代他完成了！绝不能让公义迟到！哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Now, I've done the kind deed on his behalf. Justice mustn't be delayed! Hahahaha!

t_ch: …我的时间不多了。
t_en: ...I'm running out of time.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我还以为是什么事呢。不用担心，疾风侠会替你解决的。
t_en: Hahaha, I thought it was the end of the world! Relax, I'll help you.

t_ch: 来。大声呼喊出我的名字！疾风侠，疾风——力量！
t_en: Come on! Just shout my name! Quicksilver. Speed is power!

t_ch: 疾风——力量！
t_en: Speed is power!

t_ch: 疾风——力量！！！
t_en: Speed is power!!!

t_ch: 很有气势！好，快上来吧，不管你要去哪，疾风侠都会准时抵达。
t_en: I love your energy! Come on in! I'll give you a lift to wherever you want to go.

t_ch: 不用感谢我，护送乘客是疾风侠的职责！
t_en: There's no need to thank me. Offering rides is my job!

t_ch: 要收钱吗？
t_en: Do you charge for the ride?

t_ch: 会很贵吗？
t_en: Is it expensive?

t_ch: 嗯！时间能换成金钱，这位朋友。我必须向你收取足够高昂的费用，请相信，这也是为了维护世界的正义。
t_en: Of course! Time is money, my friend. I must charge you a substantial fee, but please believe me it is to uphold the justice in the world.

t_ch: 所以我必须代为完成！绝不偏袒任何行为！哈哈哈哈。
t_en: So, I must do it for the sake of justice! No favoritism! Hahahaha!

t_ch: 对不起！我好像忘了一件极其重要的事！
t_en: Sorry, I seem to have overlooked something extremely important!

t_ch: 你把我买回来，应该花了不少钱吧。这不行，疾风侠可不能有欠于人啊。
t_en: I must have cost you an arm and a leg, right? I can't allow myself to be indebted to anyone.

t_ch: 嗯。我必须先帮你把购车的钱挣回来，守护正义的事，以后再说吧！
t_en: Now, I must help you recoup the money you spent on me first. Upholding justice has to come later.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈。疾风侠出动！疾风——力量！
t_en: Hahahahaha. Quicksilver in action! Speed is power!

t_ch: 这是挣到的车费！给你，收着吧！
t_en: Here's the fare I earn. Please keep it!

t_ch: 疾风侠出动！疾风——力量！哈哈哈哈哈，跑出租好快乐啊！
t_en: Quicksilver in action! Speed is power! Hahahahaha, it's so fun to be a cab!

t_ch: 疾风侠出动！疾风——力量……
t_en: Quicksilver in action! Speed is power!

t_ch: 好空虚啊。可恶，就没有什么我能做的事情了吗！
t_en: I feel so empty. Is there really nothing I can do!?

t_ch: 我问你，你就是我的主人吗？
t_en: Are you my owner?

t_ch: 别跟我掰扯花了多少钱，那是你付给车行的。购买凭证只能说明你的所有权，然后呢？占有了我就以为能随心所欲了吗？想得也太轻松了。
t_en: Don't bother telling me how much you have splurged on me — that's the deal between you and the dealership. What else does the proof of purchase mean other than your ownership? Do you think you are free to do anything you please with that? It's not that simple.

t_ch: 我的引擎技术来自追逐萨尔索图晨昏线的浮空城市，急刹工艺师从机动球场最尖端的漂移战靴，窗玻璃是塔利亚闪亮的沙砾，就连喷漆都是公司的旗舰新款。
t_en: My engine technology originates from a floating city on the terminator line in Salsotto. The craftsmanship of my emergency brake is inspired by the cutting-edge drift boots used in the Roboball court. My window panes are made of the shiny sand from Talia, and even my paint is the latest flagship model from the IPC.

t_ch: 汗流浃背了吧？要是你够识相的话，就打消驾驶我的念头，赶快离开。
t_en: Impressive, right? If you have enough sense, drop the idea of driving me now and make yourself scarce.

t_ch: 好的，这就点击「销毁」按钮。
t_en: Fair enough. I'll hit the "Destruct" button then.

t_ch: …别别别别。有话好好说，咱们相遇就是缘，买到我的时候你也花了不少钱不是，就这么浪费多可惜。别紧张，别冲动哈。
t_en: ...Hold on. We can talk this over. Fate has brought us together, and you have spent a fortune on me. It will be a massive waste to destroy me, right? Please calm down and don't be rash.

t_ch: 开车而已嘛，我生来就是干这个的。小意思！
t_en: You want to drive me, don't you? I was born for that. No big deal!

t_ch: 你也没给我「驾驶」选项啊。
t_en: There's no "Drive" button here.

t_ch: 啊？喂喂，这个不是我干的，别怪到我头上。真的，我发誓！
t_en: Huh? Wait, that's not my doing. I swear!

t_ch: 其实我没找到「销毁」按钮。
t_en: In fact, I didn't see the "Destruct" button too.

t_ch: 啧。原来你没这个本事啊，我在怕什么。来呀，尽管销毁我啊。
t_en: Tsk. So you're just bluffing. Why am I even scared of you? Bring it on! Destroy me all you want.

t_ch: 就喜欢你看不惯我，又拿我没办法的模样！
t_en: I enjoy watching you getting all frustrated at me but can't do anything about it.

t_ch: 好的，这就换成别的豪华飞车。
t_en: Alright. I'll trade you in for another luxury speedster.

t_ch: 哎，无聊的人…那几个我都熟得很，先说说看，你想换成哪辆飞车？
t_en: Alas, humans are so boring... I'm familiar with the other models. Which one do you want?

t_ch: 豪华飞车（黑）
t_en: Luxury Speedster (Black)

t_ch: 只会奉承谄媚的东西，嘴里吐不出几句新鲜话。没意思。
t_en: That's a glib-tongued brownnoser. It's no fun.

t_ch: 豪华飞车（金）
t_en: Luxury Speedster (Gold)

t_ch: 那辆车啊，性格不错，就是脑子不太好使，要命的话还是离远点。
t_en: Oh, that one. It has a pleasant personality, but it's not exactly the smartest car out there. Steer clear if you don't want to die.

t_ch: 豪华飞车（出租款）
t_en: Luxury Speedster (Cab)

t_ch: 它曾经也是辆正常车，出过几次事故后就变得奇怪了。
t_en: Eh, it used to work fine until a couple of accidents changed it.

t_ch: 设备都很完整，引擎也是新款，怎么就发疯成这样了呢。不明白。
t_en: It's got top-notch equipment and an engine of the latest model. How it ended up in this mess is beyond me.

t_ch: 还是不换了吧。
t_en: I'll stick with you then.

t_ch: 依我看，这群车就没个正常的。劝你别买它们啊，买了铁定吃亏！
t_en: If you ask me, none of those cars is good. You might as well pour your money into the sea!

t_ch: 我呢，虽然是贵了点，你也够不太上，但钱都花了，总得派上点用场。不妨就把我停在车位里，也算是给你撑门面了。
t_en: I actually cost more than you can afford, but since you've already paid for me, you might as well put me to some use. You can keep me in the parking spot to boost your image.

t_ch: 就这么说定了。不许反悔啊。
t_en: It's a deal. There's no backing out now.

t_ch: 我的引擎技术来自追逐萨尔索图晨昏线的浮空城市，急刹工艺师从机动球场最尖端的漂移战靴，窗玻璃是塔利亚闪亮的沙砾，就连喷漆都是……
t_en: My engine technology originates from a floating city on the terminator line in Salsotto. The craftsmanship of my emergency brake is inspired by the cutting-edge drift boots used in the Roboball court. My window panes are made of the shiny sand from Talia, and even my paint is...

t_ch: 后面的词是啥来着，怎么搞忘了。
t_en: What's the rest of my lines again? It's slipped my mind.

t_ch: 我是您购买的尊享豪华独轮飞车。很荣幸为您服务。
t_en: I'm the premium luxury monowheel speedster you purchased. It's my honor to serve you.

t_ch: 请问有什么能帮您的吗？本车设备齐全，配有最新自动驾驶系统，绝无事故风险。
t_en: How can I help you? I come with the complete set of equipment and the latest autonomous driving system. The risk of accidents is zero.

t_ch: 我们快出发。
t_en: Let's hit the road.

t_ch: 快让我上车！
t_en: Let me in now!

t_ch: 明白了。这就为您启动出行程序。
t_en: Understood. I will now initiate the travel program for you.

t_ch: …抱歉，引擎激活失败。经系统检测，您的设备电源余量过低，无法正常行驶。请您在完成充电后再尝试启动。
t_en: ...Sorry, the engine failed to start. According to the system diagnosis, your device is running low on power. Please charge your device and try again.

t_ch: 我该怎么充电？
t_en: How do I charge the car?

t_ch: 本型号独轮飞车使用特制接口，请您购买适配的充电插头进行操作。
t_en: This vehicle uses a special connector. Please purchase a suitable charging plug for charging.

t_ch: 购买方式可登录飞车官网进行查询，请勿轻信市面上的仿制产品，以免出现任何意外。感谢您的配合，祝您生活愉快。
t_en: You may purchase it on our official website. Please avoid using counterfeit products on the market to prevent any mishaps. Thank you for your cooperation.

t_ch: 有其他办法吗？
t_en: Is there anything else I can do besides charging?

t_ch: 除内置电源外，本型号独轮飞车还配备有脚动驾驶系统。
t_en: In addition to the built-in batteries, this vehicle is also equipped with a foot-operated driving system.

t_ch: 如需使用，请您预先进入车内，使劲蹬踩脚下的踏板以驱动发电设备，期间不可停止。经计算，当您的蹬踏速度接近快跑时，飞车即可开始正常行驶。
t_en: To use the system, please enter the vehicle and vigorously pedal the footboard to drive the engine. Based on our estimation, the vehicle will start moving when your pedaling speed approaches a running pace.

t_ch: 本型号独轮飞车采用开放式车厢设计，您可在蹬踩过程中轻松欣赏沿途风景，绝不会乏味。感谢您的配合，祝您生活愉快。
t_en: This vehicle has an open carriage design that allows you to admire the scenery along the way while pedaling. Your journey will never be dull. Thank you for your cooperation.

t_ch: 您的设备电源余量过低，无法正常行驶。请您在完成充电后再尝试启动。
t_en: Your device is running low on power. Please charge your device and try starting the engine again.

t_ch: 我该怎么充电？
t_en: How do I charge the car?

t_ch: 本型号独轮飞车使用特制接口，请您购买适配的充电插头进行操作。
t_en: This vehicle uses a special connector. Please purchase a suitable charging plug for charging.

t_ch: 购买方式可登录飞车官网进行查询，请勿轻信市面上的仿制产品，以免出现任何意外。感谢您的配合，祝您生活愉快。
t_en: You may purchase it on our official website. Please avoid using counterfeit products on the market to prevent any mishaps. Thank you for your cooperation.

t_ch: 有其他办法吗？
t_en: Is there anything else I can do besides charging?

t_ch: 除内置电源外，本型号独轮飞车还配备有脚动驾驶系统。
t_en: In addition to the built-in batteries, this vehicle is also equipped with a foot-operated driving system.

t_ch: 如需使用，请您预先进入车内，使劲蹬踩脚下的踏板以驱动发电设备，期间不可停止。经计算，当您的蹬踏速度接近快跑时，飞车即可开始正常行驶。
t_en: To use the system, please enter the vehicle and vigorously pedal the footboard to drive the engine. Based on our estimation, the vehicle will start moving when your pedaling speed approaches a running pace.

t_ch: 本型号独轮飞车采用开放式车厢设计，您可在蹬踩过程中轻松欣赏沿途风景，绝不会乏味。感谢您的配合，祝您生活愉快。
t_en: This vehicle has an open carriage design that allows you to admire the scenery along the way while pedaling. Your journey will never be dull. Thank you for your cooperation.

t_ch: 送我去「{TEXTJOIN#87}」。
t_en: Bring me to {TEXTJOIN#87}.

t_ch: 行啊，当然可以。要是连这点小事都做不到，我还算什么豪华飞车。
t_en: Naturally. If I can't even perform such a small task, what kind of luxury speedster would I even be?

t_ch: 坐稳咯。我们现在就出发。
t_en: Sit tight, we're setting off right now.

t_ch: 送我去「黄金的时刻」。
t_en: Bring me to the Golden Hour.

t_ch: 逛腻了，想回去了？行啊，那就走吧。
t_en: Bored with shopping and wish to return already? Fine, as you wish.

t_ch: 打开车灯。
t_en: Switch on the car's lights.

t_ch: 嗨，说实话，我自己都不知道这功能有什么用。想试试的话就随你吧。
t_en: To be honest, I don't even know what this feature does myself. If you want to try it out, go right ahead.

t_ch: 送我去「{TEXTJOIN#87}」。
t_en: Bring me to {TEXTJOIN#87}.

t_ch: …「{TEXTJOIN#87}」？那艘空中飞艇？！
t_en: ... {TEXTJOIN#87}? That airship!?

t_ch: 想去！我仰慕那里很久了，好想去看看！漂亮的飞车就该停在豪华的飞艇里！
t_en: Yes, I want to go there! I've admired that place for so long and have always wanted to take a look! A beautiful flying car like me should be parked on a luxurious airship, after all!

t_ch: 快走吧，我要等不及了！
t_en: Let's go, I can't wait any longer!

t_ch: 送我去「黄金的时刻」。
t_en: Bring me to the Golden Hour.

t_ch: 这就要走了？我还没呆够呢。哎，行吧，我是你的飞车，都听你的。
t_en: Leaving already? I wanted to stay here a while longer. Well, alright, I'm your flying car after all. Your wish is my command.

t_ch: 打开车灯。
t_en: Switch on the car's lights.

t_ch: 请说：「嗨，豪华飞车，麻烦把车灯打开。谢谢。」
t_en: Please say: "Hi, luxury speedster, please switch on the lights. Thank you."

t_ch: 信不信我直接让你熄火？
t_en: Do you want me to stall your engine?

t_ch: 啧，还真是粗暴呢。好吧好吧，都依你。
t_en: Tsk, how rude. Fine, fine, whatever you say.

t_ch: 麻烦把车灯打开。谢谢。
t_en: Please switch on the lights. Thank you.

t_ch: 你忘了说我的名字。
t_en: You forgot to say my name.

t_ch: 不过嘛，看在你这么有礼貌的份上…算了吧！这就给你打开车灯。
t_en: However, since you were so polite... Fine! I'll switch them on for you.

t_ch: 送我去「{TEXTJOIN#87}」。
t_en: Bring me to {TEXTJOIN#87}.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈。朋友，当然可以，守护人们是我义不容辞的责任啊！
t_en: Hahahaha. Of course I can, friend. After all, I am duty-bound to protect humans!

t_ch: 来吧！坐上来吧！无论何时，无论要去往哪里，我都会帮你顺利抵达！
t_en: Come and sit inside me! No matter the place, I'll ensure that you reach there on time!

t_ch: 疾风——力量！目标——「{TEXTJOIN#87}」！
t_en: Speed is power! Next stop: {TEXTJOIN#87}!

t_ch: 送我去「黄金的时刻」。
t_en: Bring me to the Golden Hour.

t_ch: 没问题！无论何时，无论要去往哪里，我都会帮你顺利抵达！
t_en: No problem! No matter the place, I'll ensure that you reach there on time!

t_ch: 疾风——力量！目标——「黄金的时刻」！
t_en: Speed is power! Next stop: Golden Hour!

t_ch: 打开车灯。
t_en: Switch on the car's lights.

t_ch: 哔哔哔哔——噗！疾风——光线！
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... Poof! Traveling at the speed of light!

t_ch: 送我去「{TEXTJOIN#87}」。
t_en: Bring me to {TEXTJOIN#87}.

t_ch: 你？那可是最豪华的飞艇，所有飞车的价钱加起来，都不够买一个车位的。
t_en: You wish to go there? You do know that your destination is a luxurious airship, right? Even the price of all flying cars combined is not enough to buy a parking bay on that airship.

t_ch: 说说看，你去那里打算做什么？
t_en: Tell me, what do you plan to do there?

t_ch: 我收到了邀请。
t_en: I received an invitation.

t_ch: 我就是「{TEXTJOIN#87}」的主人！
t_en: I own {TEXTJOIN#87}!

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 呃…你知道的，我很早时候就是你的飞车了，一直很仰慕你。至于刚才那些话，都是开玩笑的，别当真。
t_en: Ah... You know, ever since I became your flying car, I have always admired you. Regarding our earlier conversation, please be assured that I was merely joking. Don't take it to heart.

t_ch: 去「{TEXTJOIN#87}」对吧？我们现在就出发！
t_en: You wish to go to {TEXTJOIN#87}, right? In that case, let us depart right now!

t_ch: 送我去「黄金的时刻」。
t_en: Bring me to the Golden Hour.

t_ch: 好好好！坐稳，我带你过去！
t_en: Okay, okay! Sit tight, I'll take you there right now!

t_ch: 打开车灯。
t_en: Switch on the car's lights.

t_ch: 没想到吧，连我的车灯采用的都是博识学会的最新专利。睁大眼睛，做好迎接光明的准备！
t_en: Surprised? I'll have you know that even my lights are the most recent patent of the Intelligentsia Guild. Open your eyes and prepare to witness unparalleled brightness!

t_ch: 送我去「{TEXTJOIN#87}」。
t_en: Bring me to {TEXTJOIN#87}.

t_ch: 您的设备电源余量过低，无法正常行驶。请您在完成充电后再尝试启动。
t_en: Your device is running low on power. Please charge your device and try starting the engine again.

t_ch: 如急需出行，请使用其他飞车。感谢您的配合，祝您生活愉快。
t_en: If you're in a hurry, do consider using another flying car. Thank you for your cooperation and have a pleasant day ahead.

t_ch: 送我去「黄金的时刻」。
t_en: Bring me to the Golden Hour.

t_ch: 您的设备电源余量过低，无法正常行驶。请您在完成充电后再尝试启动。
t_en: Your device is running low on power. Please charge your device and try starting the engine again.

t_ch: 如急需出行，请使用其他飞车。感谢您的配合，祝您生活愉快。
t_en: If you're in a hurry, do consider using another flying car. Thank you for your cooperation and have a pleasant day ahead.

t_ch: 你一口吃掉了脆筒，酸酸甜甜的奶油在口腔飘散…味道不错。
t_en: You stuff the cone into your mouth, savoring the sour and sweet flavor of the cream... That's yummy.

t_ch: 「嘴下留情啊！」你似乎听见脆筒这么说道——但是太晚了，现在你只能回味它的甜美了。
t_en: "Please have some mercy!" You seem to hear the crisp cries of the cone, but it's too late. You can only relish its lingering taste in your mouth.

t_ch: 你吃掉了脆筒，它不算坚硬的筒身在你嘴里被切断、磨碎…真解压！
t_en: You gobble up the cone. Its crunchy shell is broken and crushed into bits in your mouth... What a satisfying experience!

t_ch: 冰冰凉凉的感觉包裹了你的舌头，没什么比酸甜可口的冷饮更清爽了。
t_en: Your tongue is enveloped by an icy sensation. Nothing is more refreshing than a sweet and sour drink served chilled.

t_ch: 酒瓶是为了供人痛饮才存在，甜筒是为了给人畅吃才存在——而你帮助它完成了毕生的使命。
t_en: Wine is made to be drunk, and the cones exist to be eaten. You have helped the cone fulfill its lifelong mission.

t_ch: 你一口将脆筒吞吃落肚，动作干净利落。
t_en: You toss a cone into your mouth in a slick move.

t_ch: 你开始思考，这座城市里的脆筒是否会有被你吃完的那天。
t_en: You begin to wonder whether you will finish up all the cones in this city one day.

t_ch: 哪怕这些脆筒修建了草房子，也拦不住胃口大开的你。
t_en: Even those houses made of cones are not spared by your ravenous appetite.

t_ch: 你又吃掉了一个脆筒，幸好在梦中吃甜食不用考虑体重负担。
t_en: You munch on yet another cone. Fortunately, being a sweet tooth in a dream doesn't make you gain weight.

t_ch: 光一个脆筒怎能满足你的胃口？你又吃下第二个脆筒，仔细地品味了一通。
t_en: How can you stop at just having one cone? You put another cone into your mouth and savor it.

t_ch: 你咀嚼着甜筒，不禁开始思考——既然街上有这么多免费的甜筒，为什么之前它们没有被吃光呢？也许这就是梦境吧。
t_en: While chewing on the cone, you wonder why the many free cones on the street haven't been eaten. It's probably only possible in a dreamscape.

t_ch: 你咽下最后一个脆筒，打了一个满意的饱嗝——在美食面前就没必要矜持了。
t_en: You swallow the last cone and let out a burp in satisfaction. There's no need to care about table manners when enjoying good food.

t_ch: 你一连吃掉了三个脆筒，甚至感觉有些饱腹…多亏梦里吃不坏肚子。
t_en: After eating three cones in a row, you start to feel a bit bloated... Luckily, you won't suffer from diarrhea in a dream.

t_ch: 随着最后一个脆筒下肚，一股若有若无的空虚从你心底泛起…难道是吃得太快了？
t_en: As you finish the last of the cone, a faint sense of emptiness sets in... Is it because you've eaten the cones too quickly?

t_ch: 当第三个脆筒吃到一半时，你感觉到了一丝发腻——或许这就是为什么厂商把一组脆筒的数量控制在三个。
t_en: When you are halfway through the third cone, you start to get sick of the taste. That's probably why the manufacturer keeps only three cones in a set.

t_ch: 在不断重复的吞吃动作中，你似乎理解了「贪饕」的命途……
t_en: While repeating the act of swallowing, you seem to gain an understanding of the Path of Voracity...

t_ch: 停！一定要把我吃掉吗？或许我们可以和平共处？
t_en: Stop! Do you really have to eat me? Perhaps, we can get along in peace.

t_ch: （一口吃掉）
t_en: (Eat it in one bite)

t_ch: 那我吃什么呢？
t_en: What am I going to eat then?

t_ch: 给、给你这个，求求你放过我……
t_en: Th—This is for you. Please let me go...

t_ch: 这次先放过你。
t_en: I'll show you mercy this time.

t_ch: 感、感谢您……
t_en: Th—Thanks...

t_ch: 这最后一个脆筒似乎有些特别——准确的说，它的形状有些不妙。不过你还没来得及感到不适，巧克力味的甜筒就已经在你嘴里融化了。
t_en: This final cone is quite special. To be more precise, it has an odd shape. Before you can feel the discomfort caused by the difference, the chocolate-flavored cone has already melted in your mouth.

t_ch: 容器的使命是存储，但这台机器选择将财富分享给世界——这或许也是「同谐」的一种表现？
t_en: The purpose of a container is storage, but this machine chooses to share its wealth with the world. Could it be an act of Harmony?

t_ch: 「你可说不准天上何时掉馅饼。」
t_en: "You can't tell when the windfall will come."

t_ch: 一声脆响过后，钞票如雪般飞落。
t_en: After a crisp sound, banknotes fall like a flurry of snow.

t_ch: 还不等你提出要取钱，钞票就从机器内喷涌而出。
t_en: Before you even request a withdrawal, money bursts forth from the machine.

t_ch: 钞票纷纷扬扬飞上了天——倘若没人拿走，打扫起来可能要费点功夫。
t_en: Banknotes flutter in the sky. If no one picks them up, cleaning the place will be a hassle.

t_ch: 你原本以为必须要念点什么咒语才行，不过现在看来，只需要站在它面前就够了。
t_en: You thought you needed to recite some spell to make the machine work, but now it seems like you only have to stand in front of it.

t_ch: 你不禁开始思考：这台机器如此慷慨地发钱，还会有宾客因捉襟见肘离开梦境吗？
t_en: With this generous machine around, will anyone still be forced to leave the Dreamscape due to financial issues?

t_ch: 答案是「有」——玩嗨了的人可不知道什么叫理性消费。
t_en: The answer is yes. People who are having a blast couldn't care less about spending wisely.

t_ch: 你和这只蓝色的小鸟面面相觑。它似乎没有要开口的意思。
t_en: You come face-to-face with a blue bird, but it does not look like it's going to speak anytime soon.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent.)

t_ch: 你们一人一鸟就这么互相盯着对方，时间在沉默中流逝。
t_en: Time passes in silence as you and the bird stare at each other.

t_ch: （还是保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 这场无声的角力还在继续。不知你们谁会是最后的胜者。
t_en: The silent staring contest continues. No one knows who's going to win.

t_ch: （继续保持沉默！）
t_en: (Continue remaining silent!)
...Hey.

t_ch: 你这家伙，看得见我吗？
t_en: Can you see me?

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 显而易见，我是只折纸小鸟，你可以叫我「湛蓝爵士」。
t_en: Obviously, I'm an origami bird. You can call me Dr. Blues.

t_ch: 什么是折纸小鸟？
t_en: What is an origami bird?

t_ch: 折纸小鸟…就是折纸做的小鸟，你没看过《钟表小子》吗？
t_en: An origami bird... is a bird made of folded paper. Haven't you watched Clockie?

t_ch: 我没空给你解释设定，自己去找{RUBY_B#折纸小鸟聚集地}「大树」{RUBY_E#}的那几只鸟问问吧。
t_en: I have no time to explain the settings. You can ask those birds on the {RUBY_B#origami birds' gathering spot}Great Tree{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 我知道了。
t_en: Got it.

t_ch: 知道就好。
t_en: Mhm, make sure you never forget about it.

t_ch: 我忙的很，有事去找{RUBY_B#折纸小鸟聚集地}「大树」{RUBY_E#}的那几只鸟吧，它们应该有事要拜托你。
t_en: I'm swamped with work. If you have any questions, you can go to those birds on the {RUBY_B#origami birds' gathering spot}Great Tree{RUBY_E#}. I think they'll need your help with their affairs.

t_ch: 等你帮它们<color=#dbc291ff>找到足够数量的折纸小鸟后，我就会给你奖励</color>——「故事情节」是这么写的。
t_en: When you have helped them <color=#dbc291ff>find the required number of origami birds, I'll give you rewards.</color> This is written under the "Storyline" section.

t_ch: 小鸟向你挥了挥翅膀，应该是让你「赶紧走」。
t_en: The birdie flaps its wings at you, supposedly trying to urge you to go.

t_en: I've met the other birdies.

t_ch: 我见过其他小鸟了。
t_en: I've met the other birdies.

t_ch: 我见过其他小鸟了。
t_en: I've met the other birdies.

t_en: But I guess you haven't <color=#dbc291ff>found all the origami birds</color> yet, right? Come back again when you've done that.

...Hey.

t_ch: 你来找我不会就为了发呆吧。
t_en: Don't tell me you're here just to zone out.

t_ch: 我完成小鸟们的委托了！
t_en: I've completed the commission for the birdies!

t_ch: 我完成小鸟们的委托了！
t_en: I've completed the commission for the birdies!

t_ch: 我完成小鸟们的委托了！
t_en: I've completed the commission for the birdies!

t_ch: 活干的还挺快。找到所有折纸小鸟了？
t_en: That's efficient. Have you found all the origami birds?

t_ch: 找鸟好累啊啾…
t_en: Finding the origami birds is exhausting, tweet...

t_ch: 小鸟好可爱啾！
t_en: The birds are so lovely, tweet!

t_ch: 嗯嗯，行，好，对对对。
t_en: Yes... Right... Sure... Exactly.

t_ch: 「这是给予勤劳的小镇居民的奖励。」那个叫「猫头鹰老师」的角色是这么说的，拿去吧。
t_en: According to Professor Owl, this is a reward for hardworking residents. You may have it.

t_ch: 好了，一切搞定。我在这集的戏份终于结束了。
t_en: Well, it's a wrap! I've finally finished my part in this episode.

t_ch: 等等，我还有问题！
t_en: Hold on. I have some questions!

t_ch: 有问题先憋着，提前剧透可不是什么好习惯。
t_en: Keep those questions to yourself for now. I don't want to give any spoilers.

t_ch: 故事距离「真相大白」的章节还早着呢。
t_en: You still have a long way to go before reaching the chapter where the truth will be revealed.

t_ch: 你为什么不会「啾」？
t_en: Why don't you "tweet" when you speak?

t_ch: …因为我不想。
t_en: ...Because I don't want to.

t_ch: 你盯着小鸟，誓要从它脸上挖出些许端倪。
t_en: You scrutinize the birdie, determined to catch some hints from its expression.

t_ch: 啾，满意了？
t_en: Tweet. Happy now?

...Hey.

t_ch: 你这家伙，在这和鸟互相瞪着有意思吗？

t_ch: 你好。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 你好。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 你好。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 哼，别想假装不认识。实在闲得慌，就去找{RUBY_B#折纸小鸟聚集地}「大树」{RUBY_E#}的那几只鸟吧，它们应该有事要拜托你。
t_en: Hmph, stop pretending we're strangers. If you're really bored, go seek out the birds at the {RUBY_B#Origami Birds' gathering spot}Great Tree{RUBY_E#}, they should have some requests to ask of you.

t_ch: 什么是折纸小鸟？

t_ch: 折纸小鸟…就是折纸做的小鸟，你没看过《钟表小子》吗？

t_en: Hold on. I have some questions!

t_ch: 有问题就憋着，什么都好奇只会害了你。
t_en: Keep your problems to yourself. Curiosity will only kill you.

t_ch: 你为什么还不「啾」？
t_en: Why aren't you tweeting yet?

t_ch: …因为我不想。
t_en: ...Because I don't want to.

t_ch: 你盯着小鸟，誓要从它脸上挖出些许端倪。

t_en: Tweet. Happy now?

t_ch: 小鸟抖了抖翅膀，转身飞走了。
t_en: The bird flaps its wings and flies off.

t_ch: 滴~嗒！灰色的朋友，我们又见面啦！
t_en: Tick-tock! Here we are again, my gray friend!

t_ch: 好不容易遇到一个能看得见我的朋友，我得好好给你科普一下美梦小镇的奥妙才行！
t_en: I rarely run into people who can see me, so I must make use of this opportunity to tell you all the wonders about Dreamville!

t_ch: 看到你面前这尊雕像了吗？不瞒你说，它就是根据我——钟表小子——的形象被打造出来的哦！
t_en: See that statue in front of you? Tell you what, it is made based on me, Clockie!

t_ch: 哇，真是太厉害啦！
t_en: Wow, that's amazing!

t_ch: 能看出来，毕竟我不瞎。
t_en: I can tell. I'm not blind, you know.

t_ch: 滴答滴答，这尊雕像的秘密可不止这些！接下来我要说的话，就只讲给能看见我的朋友听！
t_en: Tick-tock, tick-tock, there are more secrets about this statue! Next, I'm going to tell you things I only reveal to my friends!

t_ch: 你在美梦里畅游的时候，偶尔应该能拿到一种<color=#dbc291ff>金色的钞票</color>，钞票上面还印着我的头像，对吧？
t_en: You do occasionally receive a kind of <color=#dbc291ff>golden banknote</color> with my face on it during your journey in the Dreamscape, right?

t_ch: 的确拿到了一些。
t_en: I do.

t_ch: 没错没错！我就知道灰色的朋友肯定善于观察！
t_en: That's right! I knew you would be a good observer!

t_ch: 那是啥？
t_en: What's that?

t_ch: 你没见到过吗？没关系，只要<color=#dbc291ff>在梦境的世界里勤加探索</color>，你迟早会拿到它的！
t_en: Haven't you come across it? It doesn't matter. You'll get one sooner or later by <color=#dbc291ff>exploring the Dreamscape</color> more frequently!

t_ch: 接下来才是重点，你可要听好：很多人都不知道，那种金色的钞票可以被用来为「我」补充美梦能量！
t_en: Here comes the crucial part, so listen closely — many have no idea that those golden banknotes can be used to replenish my dream energy!

t_ch: 只要把那些金色的——滴答，就叫它「金表钞」吧——只要把那些「金表钞」交给这尊雕像，我在美梦小镇中的声誉和影响力就会提升！
t_en: By handing those golden banknotes — let's call it Clock Credits — to the statue, I'll become more famous and influential in Dreamville!

t_ch: 如果雕像能多多获取「金表钞」里藏着的力量，早晚有一天，美梦小镇的所有人就都能看到我的模样啦！
t_en: The more energy the statue acquires from Clock Credits, the sooner everyone in Dreamville can see me!

t_ch: 到了那个时候，我就可以跟你、还有你的伙伴们一起玩咯！
t_en: When the time comes, I'll be able to hang out with you and your buddies!

t_ch: 要是我不想让别人看见你呢？
t_en: What if I don't want the others to see you?

t_ch: 滴~嗒！真正的好朋友才不会说这种话！
t_en: Tick... tock! If you were my true friend, you wouldn't say things like that!

t_ch: 懂了，你就是贪财。
t_en: I get it. You're just hungry for the money.

t_ch: 滴~嗒！灰色的朋友，请你收回刚才说的这句话！
t_en: Tick... tock! My gray friend, please retract that statement!

t_ch: 哦，顺带一提，你在美梦小镇一般的商店里是不能用「金表钞」买东西的喔！如果你擅自把那种钞票掏出来，指不定还会被人盯上！
t_en: For your information, you can't use Clock Credits to buy stuff in regular stores in Dreamville! You might even get yourself targeted by pickpockets if you try using them in public!

t_ch: ——所以不如把它们都交给我！只要你能为我补充美梦能量，我也会给你准备丰厚的礼物的！
t_en: So, you might as well let me have them! As long as you can replenish my dream energy, I'll give you awesome gifts in return!

t_ch: 滴答，需要解释的就这么多啦！记住，<color=#dbc291ff>多多探索美梦小镇、多多赚取金表钞、然后多多把它们带到我的雕像这儿来！</color>下次见咯，灰色的朋友！
t_en: Tick-tock, that's all I want to say! Remember to <color=#dbc291ff>explore the Dreamville as much as you can, earn Clock Credits, and take them to my statue here!</color> See you, my gray friend!

t_ch: 哇哦！灰色的朋友，没想到你已经为我带来这么多「金表钞」了，真是厉害！
t_en: Wow! I'm surprised you have gathered so many Clock Credits for me, my gray friend! That's terrific!

t_ch: 虽然我现在拥有的美梦能量还不足以让小镇上的所有人都看到我…但这已经是很大的进步了！这都多亏了你，灰色的朋友！
t_en: Although I still don't have enough dream energy to make myself visible to everyone in town... this is already a significant improvement! I owe you a big one, my gray friend!

t_ch: 滴答！为了向你证明，金表钞的确能提升我在美梦小镇里的影响力——下次和雕像互动的时候，你可要睁大眼睛仔细看好咯！
t_en: Tick-tock! Pay closer attention the next time you interact with the statue. You'll see how the Clock Credits can indeed make me more influential in Dreamville!

t_ch: 下次见咯，灰色的朋友！
t_en: See you next time, my gray friend!

t_ch: 帕姆，你看看这个梦泡？
t_en: Pom-Pom, check out this dream bubble.

t_ch: 看到你手中梦泡的一瞬间，帕姆「蹭」地一下跳了起来——你从未见过它圆润的双足离开车厢地板如此之远。
t_en: Pom-Pom jumps the moment they see the dream bubble in your hands. You've never seen their chubby feet making it that far from the ground before.

t_ch: 你、你是从哪里拿到的那东西帕？！
t_en: Wh—Where did you get that thing!?

t_ch: 快、快把它拿给我看看！
t_en: L—Let me take a closer look!

t_ch: 帕姆从你手中抢走了那只梦泡，然后将它毛茸茸的额头抵了上去……
t_en: Pom-Pom yanks the dream bubble away from you and presses their furry forehead on it...
Agh...

t_ch: 别哭啊你！
t_en: Please don't cry!

t_ch: 怎么回事？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 帕姆的眼睛里似乎有什么东西在闪烁——是泪花吗？还没等你看清楚，它就用大耳朵挡住了眼睛，似乎生怕被你发现它伤感的样子。
t_en: Something is glistening in Pom-Pom's eyes. Are they tears? Before you can look closer, they cover their eyes with their large ears, trying to hide their sorrow from you.

t_ch: 几秒钟后，帕姆的心情似乎平复了下来，神态也恢复了正常。
t_en: Pom-Pom manages to pull themselves together several seconds later.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客…在这列车上发生过很多你不知道的事情呢帕。等到以后时机成熟了再和你讲吧！
t_en: {NICKNAME}... many things that have happened on this train aren't within your knowledge. I'll tell you about them when the time is right!

t_ch: 列车锅，我最中意的那口列车锅…那家伙，到底什么时候才能把它还给我呢？
t_en: Express Special... My favorite Express Special... When will that guy give it back to me?

t_ch: 滴答！{NICKNAME}，我最好的朋友——我从来都没想过，自己居然还能等到这一天的到来！
t_en: Tick-tock! {NICKNAME}, my best friend! The day I've been eagerly anticipating has finally arrived!

t_ch: 自从你来到美梦小镇以后，这里的气氛相比从前完全变了一个样！你贡献的「金表钞」让镇上的每位居民都能量满满，活力满满！
t_en: Since your arrival in Dreamville, this place has completely changed! The Clock Credits you contributed have brought energy and vitality to every resident!

t_ch: 滴答，灰色的朋友，我现在弄明白了一件事！我从前一直都想让镇上的人们看到我，承认我…但实际上，这件事好像压根没那么重要！
t_en: Tick-tock, I've realized something, my gray friend! I desired for the townspeople to acknowledge and recognize me... but now it seems it's not as significant as I once believed!

t_ch: 在遇到你之后，我终于搞懂了：珍惜自己身边的好朋友，这才是我最应该追求的事情！
t_en: After meeting you, I finally realized that cherishing my friends is the most important thing to do!

t_ch: 仓鼠球骑士、哈努兄弟、音符小姐、还有{NICKNAME}…滴答！你们就是钟表小子最珍贵的宝藏，美梦小镇上最可爱的居民！
t_en: Hamster Ball Knight, Brother Hanu, Miss Note, and you, {NICKNAME}... Tick-tock! You are all my most cherished treasures! The most beloved inhabitants of Dreamville!

t_ch: 滴答，总之，非常感谢你的贡献！下次触碰雕像的时候，记得睁大眼睛看好——我给你准备了一个大大大…大惊喜！
t_en: Tick-tock! Thank you so much for your contributions! The next time you touch the statue, keep your eyes wide open and watch closely, because I have a huge... a tremendous surprise in store for you!

t_ch: 唷嘿！真高兴能再次见到你，我的朋友！
t_en: Hi-ho! Nice to see you again, buddy!

t_ch: 我们一起经历了这么多冒险，还帮助那么多在梦里迷失的人们找回了自我…真是不可思议！这是我经历过的最棒的旅程！
t_en: We've been through all these adventures and helped so many lost people find themselves again in these dreams... It's really extraordinary! This is the best adventure I've ever been on!

t_ch: 虽然以后恐怕没法再和米沙一起玩了…但我知道，这才是他一直以来想要前往的目的地！所以，我才不会被悲伤打倒——我该打起精神，替他感到高兴才对！
t_en: Though I probably won't be able to play with Misha in the future... I know this is the destination he's always been seeking! So, I won't let sadness knock me down. I'm gonna perk up and be happy for him!

t_ch: 米沙，还有米哈伊尔，他们之所以决定建起美梦小镇，初衷一定是希望大家能带着亲朋好友一起来到这里，享受难得的欢乐时光！
t_en: The reason why Misha and Mikhail decided to build up Dreamville was the hope that everyone could bring their friends and family here to have a good time!

t_ch: 但是，他们肯定不希望所有人都沉浸在这里，忘记了前进的路在哪！谢谢你，我最好的朋友，是你帮助美梦小镇找回了它原本存在的意义！
t_en: But they definitely wouldn't want everyone to just drown in this place and forget where the road ahead is! Thank you, my best friend! You're the one who helped Dreamville re-discover its original purpose!

t_ch: 灰色的朋友，你们的旅途应该也快要迎来下一站了吧？我还有守护美梦小镇的任务在身，就不和你们一起上路了——
t_en: Gray-haired friend, your journey is approaching its next stop too, no? I still need to protect Dreamville, so I won't be going with you...

t_ch: ——但请你务必要记得钟表小子、哈努兄弟、折纸小鸟、甚至石头老板这些伙伴噢！有机会的话也记得回来看看，再顺便多给我带些金表钞来！
t_en: ...But don't forget about your friends like Clockie, Brother Hanu, the origami birds, and even Boss Stone! If you have the chance, come back and see us. And remember to bring me more Clock Credits if you can!

t_ch: 万一你们在旅途中迷了路，就呼唤「罗盘小子」的名字吧。我一定会速速赶到大伙身边，为你们精准导航！
t_en: If you ever get lost during your travels, just call for the Compassie. I'll be there in a flash and help you to navigate with great precision!

t_ch: 在那之前，我会继续以「钟表小子」的身份留在这里，守护梦境世界的快乐跟和平！滴~答！
t_en: Before that, I'll remain here as Clockie, defending the Dreamscape's happiness and peace! Tick-tock!

t_ch: 哟，快来瞧瞧！「黄金的时刻」历史上第一位被幸运派对之神眷顾的贵宾诞生了！
t_en: Yo, check this out! The first-ever guest in Golden Hour to be blessed by the Lucky Party God is here!

t_ch: 这是几个意思？
t_en: What does that mean?

t_ch: 这不是明摆着的嘛？此时此刻，您就是整个梦境十二时分里最闪耀的那颗幸运星！
t_en: Isn't it pretty obvious? You're the shiniest lucky star across all Twelve Hours in the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 太棒了，快把奖品拿来。
t_en: Sounds fabulous. Get me my prize now.

t_ch: 别那么心急，朋友！此时此刻，您就是整个梦境十二时分里最闪耀的那颗幸运星！
t_en: Don't be so impatient my friend! Right here, right now, you're the shiniest lucky star across all Twelve Hours in the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 您瞧，「知更鸟」是最近才被安装到「美梦排排乐」里的隐藏奖励机制，而您是第一个成功摇出三个「知更鸟」画像的幸运儿！
t_en: You see, the Robin is a hidden prize mechanism recently installed in Dreamy Slots, and you're the first lucky winner who gets three Robins in a row!

t_ch: 为了褒奖您的运气和…呃，十足的运气，请您收下这张「至尊幸运儿的黑卡」！
t_en: To celebrate your luck and... um, blessing, allow me to present to you this Lucky One's Black Card!

t_ch: 这卡有什么用？
t_en: What can I do with this card?

t_ch: …这当然就要靠您自己去发现了，朋友！这么安排是为了让您在获奖的兴奋之余，还能充分享受探索带来的满足感！没错，设计如此！
t_en: ...You'll have to figure it out yourself, my friend! It's arranged that way so that you can feel the excitement of winning a prize as well as enjoy the process of discovering the uses of the card! Yes, that's the concept!

t_ch: 能不能来点实际的？
t_en: Can I have something more practical?

t_ch: 呃…这么安排是为了让您在获奖的兴奋之余，还能充分享受探索带来的满足感！没错，设计如此！
t_en: Um... It's arranged that way so that you can feel the excitement of winning a prize as well as enjoy the process of discovering the uses of the card! Yes, that's the concept!

t_ch: 什么过时设计。
t_en: What an outdated concept.

t_ch: 怎么能这么说呢？这叫「经典设计」，「经典」懂哇！
t_en: How can you describe it that way? It's called a classic design. A classic, you know?

t_ch: 读道具描述是吧？老套路了。
t_en: I have to read the item description, right? That's an old trick.

t_ch: 您真是一点就通，没错，我们遵循的就是如此经典的设计套路。
t_en: I'm glad you catch my drift. That's right. We always do things here the old-school way.

t_ch: 那么，「至尊幸运儿的黑卡」还请您收下…接下来您想干什么就干什么，我就不打扰您了！
t_en: Well, please keep the Lucky One's Black Card... Now, you're free to do whatever you want. I'll get out of your hair!

t_ch: 丹西灰溜溜地离开了——看得出来，他的兴致在和你聊天的过程中被扫得一点不剩。
t_en: Dancy leaves with a crestfallen look. Apparently, your conversation with him has sapped him of his enthusiasm.

t_ch: 来自内心的声音告诉你——这扇门的背后一定隐藏着什么不得了的秘密。
t_en: Your intuition tells you there must be an incredible secret hidden behind this door.

t_ch: 来自内心的声音还告诉你——那张黑卡就是打开这扇门的钥匙。
t_en: Your intuition tells you the Lucky Guy's Black Card is the key to this door.

t_ch: …好吧，不开玩笑了，你内心压根就没那么想。你能找到这扇门靠的完全是：一、瞎蒙；二、系统提示。
t_en: ...That won't fool anyone, you've never really had those thoughts. You find this door solely relying on your random guesses and the system prompts.

t_ch: 赶紧刷卡开门吧。
t_en: Swipe the card to open the door now.

t_ch: 此处不知为何留下了一支录音笔。你摁下播放键……
t_en: There is a voice recorder here. You press the "Play" button...

t_ch: 我*皮皮西粗口*！谁让你把这房间布置成这*皮皮西粗口*样的？
t_en: What the *Pepeshi profanity*! Who asked you to set up this room in this *Pepeshi profanity* way!?

t_ch: 我们要的是奖励房、奖励房懂不懂？！不是什么情趣房间！
t_en: We wanted a prize room! You heard me!? Not a love hotel room!

t_ch: *粗口*！要怎么布置你们怎么不早提？等你们的需求提过来，道具部的采购截止日期早就过了！
t_en: *Profanity*! Why didn't you say so earlier? By the time your requests came in, the procurement deadline had long passed!

t_ch: 没有明确的需求，我当然只能先随口说说咯…这难道能怪我吗？*粗口*！
t_en: Without clear instructions, I could only do things my way... How can you blame me for that? *Profanity*!

t_ch: …录音到这里戛然而止。你猜测那两个人有大概六成的概率在这房间里打了一架。
t_en: ...The recording comes to a sudden halt. You think there is a <unbreak>60%</unbreak> chance that the two had a fight in this room.

t_ch: 欢迎光临，请问有什么需要的吗？
t_en: Welcome, how can I help you?

t_ch: 来看看吧，客人，包您满意！
t_en: Dear guest, come in and take a look, there will definitely be something you like!

t_ch: 梦境特产，如假包换。
t_en: Dreamscape's specialty, guaranteed genuine or your money back.

t_ch: 欢迎光临美梦小镇主题餐厅，请问有什么需要的吗？
t_en: Welcome to the Dreamville theme restaurant. How can I help you?

t_ch: 飞碟堡，橡木蛋糕卷，「鲱鱼先生」柠檬塔…好难选啊……
t_en: Floatdisc burgers, oak cake rolls, "Mr. Herring's" lemon tarts... I can't choose...!

t_ch: 今晚吃什么好呢…
t_en: What should I have for dinner tonight...

t_ch: 你好？
t_en: Hello?

t_ch: 不如去吃钟表披萨！
t_en: Why not have a Clockie Pizza!?
Eek!

t_ch: 咳咳…您好，尊贵的客人，欢迎来到朝露公馆。
t_en: Ahem... Hello, dear guest, welcome to the Dewlight Pavilion.

t_ch: 您与哪位「家族」成员预约了会面呢？
t_en: Which member of The Family did you schedule a visit with?

t_ch: （与「家族」成员预约…我该怎么回答呢……）
t_en: (Scheduled a meeting with a member of The Family...? How should I respond...?)

t_ch: （忽悠一下）
t_en: (Make up a meeting)

t_ch: 我要来找星期日。
t_en: I am here to see Sunday.

t_ch: 哎呀，竟然是星期日大人的客人！
t_en: Oh my! A guest to see Master Sunday!

t_ch: 我要来找知更鸟。
t_en: I am here to see Robin.

t_ch: 原来是知更鸟小姐的客人呀。
t_en: Ah, so you're here to see Miss Robin.

t_ch: 我要来找加拉赫。
t_en: I am here to see Gallagher.

t_ch: 加拉赫先生？倒是很少有来找这位先生的客人……
t_en: Mister Gallagher? Guests asking to see Mister Gallagher are rather rare...

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 难道……
t_en: Could it be...

t_ch: 难道说…您是那位大人的客人吗！他最近确实秘密接待了一些客人……
t_en: Could it be... You are a guest of the Master himself! He has indeed been receiving a number of guests in secret recently...

t_ch: 明明你没说话，但面前的迎宾员似乎却已明白了一切。
t_en: You did not say anything, but the usher before you seems to have completely understood the situation.

t_ch: 刚刚真是失礼了——您是「家族」的贵客，请向里走吧！会有人来接待您的。
t_en: I can only apologize for my rudeness before — As a VIP guest of The Family, please enter! Someone will be along to greet you shortly.

t_ch: 当然，有什么问题也可以回来找我。
t_en: Of course, if you have any issues, please feel free to come to me.

t_ch: 祝您一切顺利！
t_en: I hope you have a pleasant time!

t_ch: 我有一些疑惑。
t_en: There are some things I am confused about.

t_ch: 还有什么我能帮到您的？
t_en: Is there anything else I can help you with?

t_ch: 这是哪里？
t_en: Where is this place?

t_ch: 这里「朝露的时刻」——与匹诺康尼的大部分地区不同，这里并不对外开放，基本留给「家族」成员与少部分贵客使用。
t_en: Unlike the majority of the other districts of Penacony, the "Moment of Morning Dew" is not open to outsiders. It is fundamentally reserved for use by members of The Family and a select number of VIP guests.

t_ch: 请放心，在朝露公馆中的所有谈话都是会受到「家族」的保护，绝对保密，绝对安全！
t_en: Please rest assured that any conversations held within Dewlight Pavilion will be protected by The Family and will be kept completely confidential!

t_ch: 感觉这里戒备很严格。
t_en: I feel like security here is extremely strict?

t_ch: 没错。除了有入梦权限的「家族」成员外，就只有像您这样被直接邀请的贵宾知道抵达朝露公馆的方法。
t_en: That's correct. Aside from Family Members with dream access, only VIP guests such as yourself that have been directly invited know how to reach the Dewlight Pavilion.

t_ch: 所以这里甚至可以称为是全匹诺康尼最安全的地方——对那些擅入者来说，光是寻找进入这里的方法就得费上一番功夫了。
t_en: So, you could go as far as to say that this is the safest area in all of Penacony. Those that would try to infiltrate this place would have a hard enough time simply trying to find a way in.

t_ch: 而且公馆里也有很多安保措施。您就放一百个心吧！
t_en: What's more, there are many more security measures inside the pavilion itself. You can rest completely assured!

t_ch: 请问你如何看待美梦？
t_en: What's your take on dreams?

t_ch: 哎呀，还从没有来客问过我这种问题呢。
t_en: Oh my, I have never had a guest ask me a question like that.

t_ch: 我想想…对大多数人来说，这片美梦应该是当之无愧的天堂。
t_en: Let me think... For the majority of people, this wonderful dream would be an absolute paradise.

t_ch: 但我从小在这里长大，再美的景色看多了也容易觉得单调…偶尔我也会想去看看匹诺康尼外的世界，看看那种挂着群星的天空。
t_en: However, having grown up here, even the most breathtaking sights can grow dull and monotonous... Sometimes I wish to visit the world outside Penacony and see the starry sky of reality.

t_ch: 相信您一定见过那样的银河吧。
t_en: I'm sure you have laid eyes on the cosmos of which I speak?

t_ch: 没什么了。
t_en: Um... Nothing.

t_ch: 好的，祝您一梦平…啊不对，一梦愉快！
t_en: Very well, I wish you a safe drea — Ah, I mean a joyous dream!

t_ch: 我没有想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing to ask.

t_ch: 好的，祝您一梦平…啊不对，一梦愉快！
t_en: Very well, I wish you a safe drea — Ah, I mean a joyous dream!

t_ch: 为您服务是我的荣幸——祝您一切顺利！
t_en: It is my honor to be able to serve you. I wish you well!

t_ch: 飞碟堡，橡木蛋糕卷，「鲱鱼先生」柠檬塔……今晚吃什么好呢？
t_en: Floatdisc burgers, oak cake rolls, "Mr. Herring's" lemon tarts... What should I have for dinner tonight...?

t_ch: 公馆的工作太简单了，每天上班就只想着「今天吃什么」……
t_en: Work at the pavilion is so easy... all I think about while I'm at work is "what should I have for dinner tonight..."

t_ch: 哎，布琳娜，你不能再这么堕落下去了。该认真工作了！
t_en: Ah, Brina! You must not let yourself slack off like this. Pay attention to your work!

t_ch: …嗯，今晚还是去吃钟表披萨吧。
t_en: ...Hmm, maybe I will have a Clockie Pizza for dinner tonight.
Alas...

t_ch: 公馆很少有人拜访，这份工作倒是难得清闲…但有的时候也让人难以忍耐……
t_en: The pavilion rarely has visitors, so this job offers a lot of leisure time... But sometimes it can be unbearable...

t_ch: 毕竟——只有一个人站岗，能和我聊天的人实在太少了！
t_en: I'm working here alone, after all. There's barely anyone to talk to!

t_ch: 偶尔来了客人也都是非富即贵的大人物，哪有人愿意和一个小小的迎宾员聊天呢……
t_en: When we do get the odd visitor, they're all extremely rich and important figures. Why would any of them want to talk to a lowly usher...

t_ch: 哎，大概这就是清闲的代价吧……
t_en: Ah, I guess this is the cost of having a leisurely job...

t_ch: 只有一个人站岗，能和我聊天的人实在太少了！
t_en: I'm working here alone. There's barely anyone to talk to!

t_ch: 哎，大概这就是清闲的代价吧……
t_en: Ah, I guess this is the cost of having a leisurely job...

t_ch: 您评评理——哪能有这种事儿呢！
t_en: You tell me... How could such a thing happen!?

t_ch: 好不容易上岸一个清闲又稳定的岗位——没想到居然要我之后去「安谧的时刻」轮岗！
t_en: I worked so hard to get myself into this nice, stable, leisurely position... Then I find out that they wanna reassign me to shifts in the "Moment of Serenity!"

t_ch: 据说那里关押的都是最最可怕的犯人……
t_en: I hear that all of the most terrifying convicts are held there...

t_ch: 据说有伪装成熊崽的智械、自称为皇帝的游民，甚至某次似乎还有押进来个奇怪的广告牌……
t_en: I hear there is an Intellitron that impersonates a teddy bear, a homeless man that calls themselves an "Emperor"... I hear they even brought in a weird billboard...

t_ch: 听着越来越离谱了…
t_en: This list just gets weirder and weirder...

t_ch: 但他们都这么说！
t_en: But that's what they all say!

t_ch: 据说、据说我的上一任还遇到过会说话的垃圾桶…！
t_en: I hear the person working there before me even met a talking trashcan!

t_ch: 这过的都是什么日子啊！
t_en: What a horrible life it would be!

t_ch: …嘿嘿，嘿嘿嘿……
t_en: ...Hehe, hehehe...

t_ch: 很久没有遇到愿意和我聊天的好心客人了。
t_en: It's been a while since I met a kind guest willing to talk with me.

t_ch: 您知道吗？即使在朝露公馆中，也有许多神秘的「未解之谜」……
t_en: Did you know that even the Dewlight Pavilion contains mysterious "unexplained riddles..."?

t_ch: 絮絮叨叨的布琳娜拉着你聊了许久，从公馆内部的神秘通道、宝库里被隐藏的小门，再到天天游荡在建筑中的恐怖幽灵……
t_en: Brina the chatterbox talks your ear off for quite some time. She talks about the mysterious passages in the building, the hidden door in the treasury, and the terrifying ghosts that roam within the structure every day...

t_ch: 你被迫听了许多不可告人的秘密八卦。希望这些故事中的当事人不会因此来找你麻烦。
t_en: You are forced to listen to loads of secret gossip that you cannot share with anyone. Let's hope that the people implicated in these stories don't come to find you and settle the score later!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，好久没有过这样酣畅淋漓的对话了。
t_en: Hehe, it's been so long since I've been able to chat to my heart's content like this.

t_ch: 不过我告诉您的那些八卦——您记得为我保密呀！
t_en: Although, these rumors I've told you... You have to keep them a secret!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，好久没有过这样酣畅淋漓的对话了。
t_en: Hehe, it's been so long since I've been able to chat to my heart's content like this.

t_ch: 不过我告诉您的那些八卦——您记得为我保密呀！
t_en: Although, these rumors I've told you... You have to keep them a secret!

t_ch: （想办法蒙混过关吧……）
t_en: (Better think of a way to fool my way through...)

t_ch: 你好，逐梦客。我是康娜，朝露公馆的告解侍者。
t_en: Hello, Dreamchaser. My name is Conna, a Confessor of Dewlight Pavilion.

t_ch: 虽然在才干上不及橡木家系的「铎音」，我依旧愿意充当希佩的代言人，聆听并赦免你的全部罪过。
t_en: I might not be able to stand toe-to-toe with the Bronze Melodia of the Oak Family, but I am more than willing to be Xipe's representative, to listen to you and forgive all your sins.

t_ch: 倘若你有愧，便说出来吧。
t_en: If you have any guilt you carry, then speak.

t_ch: 她的表情美丽而冷漠，仿佛能拒人于千里之外。你实在没法鼓起勇气对她自述「罪行」——假如你确实有的话。
t_en: Her expression is beautiful but aloof, as if she is keeping everyone at arm's length. You simply cannot muster your courage to tell her your sins — that is, if you actually had any.

t_ch: 你愿倾听我的告解吗？
t_en: Will you listen to my confession?

t_ch: 当然，有什么话就说出来吧——我会全盘接受的。
t_en: Of course. Speak freely. I will gladly receive them all.

t_ch: 我有要坦白的罪过。
t_en: I have a sin to confess.

t_ch: 那么，请你详述曾经犯下的错误，以此展现改正的决心。
t_en: Then, please elaborate on your previous indiscretion, and display your willingness to repent.

t_ch: 我经常刁难垃圾桶。
t_en: I regularly make life difficult for trashcans.

t_ch: 我对垃圾桶怀有无限的喜爱，曾在多个世界向它动手动脚，窃取其中的宝物……
t_en: I have an unfathomable love for trashcans, having fondled them all across many different worlds. I also stole plenty of valuables from inside them...

t_ch: 我也曾与有手有脚的垃圾桶大打出手，暴力推翻了他们的王。直到今天，依旧有桶视我为敌。
t_en: I also once hit a trashcan that had arms and legs, thrashing their leader thoroughly and flipping it upside down. Even now, there are trashcans that bear great hostility towards me.

t_ch: 为了奖励，我不择手段。
t_en: I have employed clandestine means for rewards.

t_ch: 我控制不住自己的双手，经常攻击路边的各种容器，将它们残忍地打碎……
t_en: I can't control my own hands, regularly attacking all kinds of containers and vessels along my path, smashing them to smithereens...

t_ch: 每到一个地方，我就要四处寻找宝箱，将里面的财宝尽数掠走，无论它们是否属于我。
t_en: Every new place I'm at, I will scour the area for chests and plunder all the valuables in them, even if they don't belong to me.

t_ch: 我消耗过崇高道德的赞许。
t_en: I have consumed the Praise of High Morals.

t_ch: 我曾多次向心中的邪念屈服，做出违反道德的事情，包括但不限于涂改留言、寄回作业、打捞喷泉……
t_en: Many times, I gave in to my evil thoughts, performing deeds that were immoral, including but not limited to altering messages, sending back homework, salvaging coins from fountains...

t_ch: 好在我拥有崇高道德的赞许，这才下得去手。
t_en: It was only because I had the Praise of High Morals that I could do all these.

t_ch: 我在此向你告解，请求弥补我的罪过。
t_en: I hereby confess to you, to please forgive my sins.

t_ch: 只要你心怀虔诚，誓不再犯，神主便会看在迷途知返的份上赐你赦免。
t_en: As long as your heart is devoted, and you vow never to commit these crimes again, the Great One will grant you forgiveness on account of you recognizing how misguided you were.

t_ch: 回去之后，记得要对同伴展露真心，对生人展现热情，如此便能抵消过错。
t_en: When you return, you must remember to treat your companions earnestly, to spread kindness to strangers — then can you atone for your mistakes.

t_ch: …流程结束。不得不说，你的经历…确实让我另眼相看。
t_en: ...The process is complete. I have to say, your experiences... do paint you in a new light.

t_ch: 你真的可以让希佩原谅我吗？
t_en: Can you really get Xipe to forgive me?

t_ch: 实际上，我并不能恳请祂与我等同在。但我愿意倾听你的话语。
t_en: Truthfully, I cannot plead with THEM to stoop down to our level. But I am willing to listen to your words.

t_ch: 大部分人的过错都只是些琐事，并没有严重到需要神主亲自赦免的地步——大家只是缺少一个倾诉的对象而已。
t_en: Most people's mistakes are but trivial, and do not warrant the Great One's personal forgiveness. They mostly just lack a listening ear.

t_ch: 而且很多时候，仪式感也是必不可少的。退一万步讲，我至少可以代表自己原谅你啊。
t_en: And many times, ceremony is also an essential part of it. If nothing else, at least I can forgive you on behalf of myself.

t_ch: 请问你如何看待美梦？
t_en: What's your take on dreams?

t_ch: 美梦…我个人倒是没什么不满。不过倾听的告解多了，难免会被某些负面情绪影响。
t_en: Dreams... I personally have no qualms against them, but one can't avoid the effects of certain negative emotions after listening to so many confessions.

t_ch: 即使家族内部，也有不少人对梦中的混乱心存芥蒂，而这个问题在短时间内无法根治。
t_en: Even within The Family, there are many who bear resentment towards the chaos in the dreams, and this problem won't be resolved anytime soon.

t_ch: 有人说，这是家族放任导致的乱象——因为我们不能对来宾做出过分的惩罚，最多也只是禁止他们再度入梦。
t_en: Some say that this madness was caused by The Family's neglect, but we can't mete punishment that is too severe on the guests — the worst we can do is to prevent them from entering the dreams again.

t_ch: 可眼下还有更好的办法吗？难道我们要为了所谓的安宁，放弃追求福祉、同谐同乐的底线吗？
t_en: But what other better solution is there? Are we supposed to sacrifice our goal of pursuing happiness and harmony, all for the so-called peace?

t_ch: 我不认为那是个好主意，匹诺康尼的梦中不应存在压迫。倘若一定要用极端手段约束来宾，那我宁愿这里永远混乱下去。
t_en: I don't think it was a good idea. There should be no suppression in Penacony's dreams. If we had to use extreme methods to regulate guests, I'd rather this place remain forever chaotic.

t_ch: 感谢你的倾听，再见。
t_en: Thank you for listening. Goodbye.

t_ch: 再见，愿你在美梦中同享喜乐。
t_en: Goodbye, and may you have joy and happiness in the dream.

t_ch: 从理论上讲，你有权向她做出告解，但她冷若冰霜的表情让你打消了这个想法…或许应该等她心情好点再说？
t_en: In theory, you have the right to give her a confession, but her frosty visage quashes any such notions... Perhaps come back when she's in a better mood?

t_ch: 一愣过后，康娜的面容重归严肃，看不出丝毫情绪的波动。
t_en: After a momentary shock, Conna's face reverts to her usual solemnity, with not a sliver of emotion on it.

t_ch: 你是来告解的吗？如果不是的话还请不要于此逗留，你身后还有其他人在等待。
t_en: Are you here for a confession? Please do not loiter if you aren't. There are people waiting behind you.

t_ch: 我终究不是真正的「铎音」，没法将告解者的苦恼尽数消化……
t_en: Ultimately, I'm not a true Bronze Melodia, and am unable to fully assuage all the burdens of those who confess...

t_ch: 啊…我终究没法像真正的「铎音」那样，将告解者的苦恼尽数消化……
t_en: Ah... I'm ultimately not a true Bronze Melodia, and am unable to fully assuage all the burdens of those who confess...

t_ch: 上次有人来找我倾诉，说他沉迷梦境，连着疯玩了好几个月；等回到现实才发现，自己远在天外的年迈养母已经因病离世，而自己错过了她的最后一面……
t_en: The last time someone came for confession, he said he was obsessed with the Dreamscape, running amok inside for months. Only when he returned to reality did he discover that his own foster mother at home, far away from him, had passed away from illness, and he had missed the chance to see her for the last time...

t_ch: 他懊恼又自责，几乎要失去活着的勇气。可我除了不痛不痒的安慰以外，什么也给不了他……
t_en: He was both upset and remorseful, almost to the point where he lost the courage to live. Yet I couldn't do anything more for him, apart from empty words of comfort...

t_ch: 这样的事情我已见过许多。比起悲伤，我心中更多的是…疲倦。
t_en: I've seen many such incidents. Compared to sorrow, my heart is more... tired.

t_ch: 这片美梦之所以能够成形，兴许就是因为它能隔绝与它不相干的一切痛苦烦忧——可谁能想到呢？它们并不会凭空消失……
t_en: Perhaps this dream was able to take shape because it can sever all pains and worries irrelevant to the dream. But who would have guessed that such anguish would not just disappear into thin air...

t_ch: 啊…我终究没法像真正的「铎音」那样，将告解者的苦恼尽数消化……
t_en: Ah... I'm ultimately not a true Bronze Melodia, and am unable to fully assuage all the burdens of those who confess...

t_ch: 上次有人来找我倾诉，说他沉迷梦境，连着疯玩了好几个月；等回到现实才发现，自己远在天外的年迈养母已经因病离世，而自己错过了她的最后一面……
t_en: The last time someone came for confession, he said he was obsessed with the Dreamscape, running amok inside for months. Only when he returned to reality did he discover that his own foster mother at home, far away from him, had passed away from illness, and he had missed the chance to see her for the last time...

t_ch: 他懊恼又自责，几乎要失去活着的勇气。可我除了不痛不痒的安慰以外，什么也给不了他……
t_en: He was both upset and remorseful, almost to the point where he lost the courage to live. Yet I couldn't do anything more for him, apart from empty words of comfort...

t_ch: 这样的事情我已见过许多。比起悲伤，我心中更多的是…疲倦。
t_en: I've seen many such incidents. Compared to sorrow, my heart is more... tired.

t_ch: 这片美梦之所以能够成形，兴许就是因为它能隔绝与它不相干的一切痛苦烦忧——可谁能想到呢？它们并不会凭空消失……
t_en: Perhaps this dream was able to take shape because it can sever all pains and worries irrelevant to the dream. But who would have guessed that such anguish would not just disappear into thin air...

t_ch: 暴力…是对美梦的背弃！
t_en: Violence... is a betrayal to dreams!

t_ch: 康娜的脸上横添了一抹怒纹，额头紧皱成一团——这是洁癖者看到一地垃圾时的正常反应。
t_en: An angry frown streaks across Conna's face, her forehead scrunched up into wrinkly folds — that is the default reaction of someone obsessed with cleanliness who sees trash strewn all about.

t_ch: 人之无情，竟可以到这种地步？真是令人作呕！
t_en: Is the heartlessness of humanity truly capable of such atrocity? It simply makes me nauseous!

t_ch: 发生甚么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 我想起了一位告解者，他向我寻求良心的慰藉，可他所做的分明是丧尽良心之事。
t_en: I'm reminded of a penitent who sought my comfort of his conscience, but what he did was clearly unconscionable.

t_ch: 家族的邀请遍及寰宇、明察秋毫，所赠的都是够格之人。而这位告解者德行不佳，才干亦不见长，并没有收到邀请。
t_en: The Family's invites were sent all over the worlds, and their judgment was keen-sighted — all who received them were worthy. But this penitent's morals and talents were both lacking, which is why he did not receive one.

t_ch: 谁曾想他被嫉妒占据了头脑，居然在半路截杀了一位来宾，夺取了对方的邀请函……
t_en: Who knew envy would take over his mind, intercepting and killing a guest on the way, then stealing the victim's invitation...

t_ch: 我当即呼叫了猎犬的家人们将他拿下，永远逐出梦境。
t_en: I summoned the Bloodhounds instantly to arrest him and exiled him from the Dreamscape forever.

t_ch: 被捕之后，他对自己的所作所为全无悔意，反倒咒骂我是背信弃义之人。
t_en: After his capture, he was remorseless about his vile act, and even cursed me for being someone who had forsaken my professional oath.

t_ch: 真是可笑——明明从选择付诸暴力的那时起，他就已经先背弃了美梦。
t_en: What a joke. He was the one who forsook the dream first the moment he chose violence.

t_ch: 康娜的脸上横添了一抹怒纹，额头紧皱成一团——这是洁癖者看到一地垃圾时的正常反应。
t_en: An angry frown streaks across Conna's face, her forehead scrunched up into wrinkly folds — that is the default reaction of someone obsessed with cleanliness who sees trash strewn all about.

t_ch: 我想起了一位告解者，他向我寻求良心的慰藉，可他所做的分明是丧尽良心之事。
t_en: I'm reminded of a penitent who sought my comfort of his conscience, but what he did was clearly unconscionable.

t_ch: 家族的邀请遍及寰宇、明察秋毫，所赠的都是够格之人。而这位告解者德行不佳，才干亦不见长，并没有收到邀请。
t_en: The Family's invites were sent all over the worlds, and their judgment was keen-sighted — all who received them were worthy. But this penitent's morals and talents were both lacking, which is why he did not receive one.

t_ch: 谁曾想他被嫉妒占据了头脑，居然在半路截杀了一位来宾，夺取了对方的邀请函……
t_en: Who knew envy would take over his mind, intercepting and killing a guest on the way, then stealing the victim's invitation...

t_ch: 我当即呼叫了猎犬的家人们将他拿下，永远逐出梦境。
t_en: I summoned the Bloodhounds instantly to arrest him and exiled him from the Dreamscape forever.

t_ch: 被捕之后，他对自己的所作所为全无悔意，反倒咒骂我是背信弃义之人。
t_en: After his capture, he was remorseless about his vile act, and even cursed me for being someone who had forsaken my professional oath.

t_ch: 真是可笑——明明从选择付诸暴力的那时起，他就已经先背弃了美梦。
t_en: What a joke. He was the one who forsook the dream first the moment he chose violence.

t_ch: 来啊，迷途的羔羊，向我敞开你的心扉吧。
t_en: Come, lost lamb, open your heart to me.

t_ch: 康娜的脸上闪过一丝笑意。她本想遮掩，却忍俊不禁。
t_en: A smile flashes across Conna's lips. She initially tries to hide it, but can't help it nonetheless.

t_ch: 噗嗤，年度模范侍者…整得就像什么大奖一样。
t_en: Psh, model attendant of the year... Like it's some sort of grand award.

t_ch: 你还是个模范？
t_en: You've won an award?

t_ch: 是啊，也许家主应该给我发个胸牌，上面写着「年度模范在此」。这样还算有些仪式感。
t_en: Yes. Perhaps the head of The Family should confer me with a medal, with "I'm the role model of the year" engraved on it. At least there'd be some sense of ceremony to it.

t_ch: 知道模范怎么评的吗？每位家人都会被发到一个沾满奶油的甜甜圈，好把它扔到自己推荐的人身上。谁被沾了最多的奶油，谁就是年度模范。
t_en: Do you know how this prize is decided? Every Family member will be sent a donut doused in cream to be thrown at the person they wish to recommend. Whoever gets most covered in cream will be the model employee of the year.

t_ch: 真是顶高帽啊。不过既然大家这么看重我，我也得好好回应才行。来吧，尽情朝我发射甜甜圈——我是说，尽情向我倾诉吧。
t_en: What a pretentious farce. But since everyone thinks so highly of me, I have to reciprocate in kind. Come, fling donuts at me to your heart's content— Err, I mean, confess to me to your heart's content.

t_ch: 如此看来，她还是有一丝幽默感的…也许只是有些怕生？
t_en: At least she still has a sense of humor... Or maybe she's just somewhat uncomfortable with new people?

t_ch: 没什么想告解了。
t_en: I've got nothing to confess anymore.

t_ch: 好，我会始终在此，等待倾听你的心声。
t_en: All right. I'll be right here, waiting to listen to your heart's voice.

t_ch: 你好啊，我是艾薇拉，《钟表小子》系列动画的忠实粉丝，也是在这里帮工的志愿者。
t_en: Greetings, I am Elvira, a loyal fan of the Clockie cartoon series, and also a volunteer here.

t_ch: 如你所见，克劳克影视乐园还有很多地方不够完善，主要是人手太有限了…当然， 我们有在努力改进。
t_en: As you can see, there are many aspects of Clock Studios Theme Park that aren't up to the task yet, mainly because we severely lack manpower... Of course, we're working on improving that.

t_ch: 总之，欢迎你到处转转。只有人多起来，乐园才能更加兴旺嘛。
t_en: In any case, you're welcome to look around. Theme parks only get more prosperous with crowds.

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 好啊，我很高兴有人能对影视乐园感兴趣。
t_en: Sure, I'm very glad that there are people interested in theme parks.

t_ch: 你为什么在这做志愿者？
t_en: Why are you volunteering here?

t_ch: 因为我想和钟表小子一起工作？还记得我小时候每天最期待的事，就是提前坐在客厅的沙发上，等着准时收看钟表小子动画。
t_en: Because I wanted to work with Clockie? I still remember when I was younger, the thing I most looked forward to every day was to sit myself in front of the living room sofa and wait for Clockie's cartoon to air.

t_ch: 可惜不管它更新得再快，也跟不上我追剧的速度。到离开校园的时候，我已经把所有钟表小子相关的衍生剧集都刷过至少三遍了。
t_en: It's a pity that no matter how fast it got updated, it could never catch up to the speed at which I watched. By the time I left school, I'd already seen all of Clockie's related spin-offs at least three times over.

t_ch: 所以，我决定亲自参与到钟表小子的制作中，哪怕只是帮大家端茶送水也好…毕竟那可是我的童年啊。
t_en: So, I decided to personally participate in Clockie's production, even if it was just serving the crew drinks and food... I mean, it was my childhood after all.

t_ch: 你在这里都做些什么？
t_en: What do you do here?

t_ch: 那可就多了，像什么帮客人拍照、跑腿，管理周边商品，修缮游乐设施之类的，偶尔还得照顾扮演动漫形象的同事。
t_en: Oh, I do plenty, like helping guests take photos, running errands, managing the merch, fixing rides and all that. Sometimes, I even have to take care of my coworkers who play the cartoon characters.

t_ch: 虽然大部分是些没什么技术含量的杂活，但架不住量大类多，大家都会时不时招呼我去帮忙。
t_en: Most are odd jobs that don't require much skill, but there's plenty of it to go around. Most people call for my help regularly.
